message:
fix(core): enable I/O reactor and Cx context in block_on (#18)

The #[tool] macro generates nested block_on calls for async handlers,
but the runtime was constructed without an I/O reactor and without
installing a Cx context. This caused two problems:

1. RuntimeBuilder::current_thread() creates a runtime with reactor: None
   and io_driver: None. Without an epoll/kqueue reactor, socket readiness
   events never arrive, so any real network I/O hangs indefinitely.

2. Runtime::block_on never calls Cx::set_current(), so Cx::current()
   returns None. Asupersync networking primitives look up the I/O driver
   via Cx::current().io_driver_handle(), and without it they fall back
   to a busy-spin/deadlock path.

Fix: create a platform reactor via create_reactor() and pass it to the
builder with .with_reactor(). Before each block_on call, install a Cx
carrying the IoDriverHandle so networking futures can discover it.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
